mechdata.mech_description: Description
mechdata.improved_agility: Added mechano-muscles, more flexible joints, or additional programming that makes the Mech move more smoothly and quickly.

mechdata.color_scheme: Burnished orange armor, warm gold highlights, iridescent scarab tones, dark graphite structure
mechdata.mech_description: Khepri Dawnfire carries the look of sunrise forged into warsteel, clad in burnished orange plates. A manta-shaped sensor helm sits low between the pauldrons, its green optics sharp and alert. The khopesh in its hand is lean and lethal, iridescent like a scarab wing, and plates on the mech's torso mirror this iridescence. The left arm is more heavily armored than the sword arm, making it a weapon in and of itself.
mechdata.sensor_suite: An enhanced predictive sensor suite to speed reactions and spot targets more quickly. (+2 Initiative)

mechdata.mech_description: TBD
mechdata.enhanced_melee: Monomolecular blades, rocket-assist fists, and other enhanced melee Mech weapons.

mechdata.color_scheme: Steely gray with red slash along the left forearm.
mechdata.mech_description: Tall, with a narrow, waspish waist and heavy shoulders, this Omicron Mech has a heavy roll of armor around the head and neck, bunching its shoulders. It's arms are lined with segmented plates and end in heavy, claw-tipped hands. The forearms are bulked up with extendable armor, allowing it to deploy a pair of shields at need. The left forearm has a slash of red paint running along it, stark against the otherwise gunmetal gray of its armor. The legs are thick and powerful, a pair of articulated toes at the front of its feet to give it better grip, and one at the heel to help it move quickly. The neat circles of covered rocket launchers pepper the pectorals, splashed far and wide across the broad chest.
mechdata.enhanced_melee: Monomolecular blades, rocket-assist fists, and other enhanced melee Mech weapons.

mechdata.mech_description: Berserker Geist is an armored behemoth, standing like it might be the patron god of the Scutarius mechs. He's a chonky boy, built to take punishment with his thick armor. He's painted charcoal gray and crimson, giving him a rather nefarious look. From the horned helm looking head to the pauldrons and the boot-like plating, it has a definite Viking flavor. Rather than a sword, it has a vicious axe that sports massive serrations along the blade. The shield is huge, done in a teardrop shape, with massive blades making up the bottom section. Jutting from the abdomen are twin gattling blasters, keeping the wide body's symmetry
mechdata.advanced_targeting_array: An advanced targeting array that increases accuracy but takes an extra moment to lock on. (+2 Accuracy, -1 Initiative)

mechdata.mech_description: The Optio Imperator looks, in a word, feral. From its slightly hunched-forward posture to the way the cannon-fingers of its right hand are frozen as if in spasm, the mech looks a moment from unleashing nightmarish violence. The Omicron mech’s royal purple-and-gold coloring does nothing to disguise the fact that it is a machine built for the singular purpose of war. Even the armor itself is somehow violent, all sharp angles and vicious points. Its weapons have been crafted almost reverently, as if to appease the rabid machine, the gunmetal left bare save for gold filigree. The machine’s slouch leaves the mouth of the plasma cannon on its back facing forward, unable to bear not keeping a weapon at the ready.
mechdata.explosive_ammo: Explosive ammo, wide-spectrum lasers, or shrapnel-laden warheads that increase damage done but have lesser penetration.

mechdata.mech_description: Deep Blue is a heavy boi. Designed for tanking and gunnery, the exterior of the unit has solid plating to help it take a beating. Shoulder mounted gatling blasters are hidden until shoulder plating lifts to allow them to fire. A turbo laser is wielded on one arm and a plasma destroyer on the other, packing some serious firepower in its one-two punch. Steel blue and graphite plating give it a sort of deep sea vibe, as do some of the smoother curves of its design, even as a biped.
mechdata.advanced_targeting_array: An advanced targeting array that increases accuracy but takes an extra moment to lock on. (+2 Accuracy, -1 Initiative)

mechdata.mech_description: A towering ultratech warrior, all hard planes and acute angles, the Valkyrie is the personification of Persepolitan knighthood writ larger than life. Like a futuristic reimagining of lost Terra's late-medieval European warriors, she stands tall and straight and true, gleaming in the light of sun or stars alike. Built for speed, Valkyrie recalls not so much a human form as an armored one, with jutting, ablative plating covering every vital system and projecting at every joint calling to mind pauldrons, bracers, greaves and even wings. Directional thrusters are set at key points to aid in a full range of motion, but what truly draws the eye is how it bristles with weaponry. Missile racks sit upon broad shoulders and rocket pods are embedded in its chest, while a massive, brushed-steel railgun is held like some giant's automatic rifle in the right hand. A bright white is the mech's primary raiment, accented at points with black piping and a radiant Aurelion gold. The left shoulder is pure black, save for the Jolly Roger, the ancient skull-and-crossbones logo once worn by Jake Tyson's flight squadron... and spelling out death to their enemies.
mechdata.explosive_ammo: Explosive ammo, wide-spectrum lasers, or shrapnel-laden warheads that increase damage done but have lesser penetration.

mechdata.mech_description: Dark blue and black, a mix of the deep waters of Yangtze and the darkness of space. It’s a fast mech, made of lighter composites, thinner armor, and weaker internals but more maneuverability. The addition of the enhanced hydraulics makes it very agile. Held in the right hand is the Plasma Destroyer, the thick energy feed cables linking it to the right arm, sourcing its power from the mech’s main reactor. Over both shoulders are twin turbolasers, folded against the back when not deployed but swivels into position when it is ready for use. Last but not least, a mech shield attached to its left arm, a last resort when he needs to step in and help hold the line. The features are visibly humanoid and bipedal, though on the back sprouts what appears to be two small wings. Not close to being flight capable but the design is displaying a yearning to sour into the sky and beyond. To fly free.
mechdata.improved_agility: Added mechano-muscles, more flexible joints, or additional programming that makes the Mech move more smoothly and quickly.

mechdata.color_scheme: Bare metal gray and uranium yellow
mechdata.mech_description: A hulking figure of bare metal gray and uranium yellow, it stands on the upper end of the Omicron size range, much of its bulk is concentrated on its upper torso. The arms are nearly as thick as its torso - in the right hand, a mace of gargantuan proportion is held in its right hand. The head is a similar gray to the mech itself, rows of serration line the dense ball. In the left hand, a tower shield stands at the ready - as of yet unmarked by battle. A cowl forms from its shoulders, encapsulating the sensor array that makes up its head - perfunctorily humanoid.
mechdata.enhanced_melee: Monomolecular blades, rocket-assist fists, and other enhanced melee Mech weapons.

mechdata.mech_description: Description
mechdata.jump_jets: Using jump jets to attack a target with melee weapons from above generates a great deal of power, but can be hard to aim.

mechdata.mech_description: This mech is a slim, agile monstrosity of pale gray armor with darker gray and luminous teal accents. They are lean and quick on their feet, able to move swiftly through most terrains while also maintaining a strict at-ranged protocol. When activated off their left gauntlet, a shield of teal springs forth to protect them from assaults.
mechdata.advanced_targeting_array: An advanced targeting array that increases accuracy but takes an extra moment to lock on. (+2 Accuracy, -1 Initiative)

mechdata.mech_description: Pale Rider's armor is a striking mosaic of ivory, gray, and subtle gold accents, dominated by pale plating that forms the primary shell, with precise geometric lines - angular, smooth, clean. Layers of matte gray components reinforce the joints and core structure, adding further depth, dimension and mechanical strength to its frame; not too heavy and not too flimsy. Its gilded details trace along the helmet, shoulders, and weapon housing, polished metal catching the light - similar in appearance to the elite, ceremonial monoliths typically integrated in public Persepolan affairs, but nothing that treads into their archetypal ostentation. The helmet is sleek and visor-like, housing advanced optics and targeting systems, while the segmented torso hints at powerful internal actuators. Precision engineering is apparent on long armored limbs, ending in reinforced boots equipped with glowing thrusters that emit soft blue-white light.
mechdata.sensor_suite: An enhanced predictive sensor suite to speed reactions and spot targets more quickly. (+2 Initiative)

mechdata.color_scheme: Matte black with red/white piping and subdued yellow indicators
mechdata.mech_description: Lean, spare humanoid mech. Finely engineered, brutally utilitarian. Nothing extraneous. It resembles a human body because the bipedal form is an efficient solution to problems of leverage, balance, and force transmission in uncertain conditions. The frame is light. Armor, pared back to what is necessary. Ribs of composite struts, tension members, actuator housings visible, accessible. Clean, well-maintained. A restrained color scheme dominated by matte black, broken by sharp runs of white-and-red piping that trace power flows, stress points, and access paths with diagrammatic confidence. Narrow torso, forward-canted, built to protect the computational apparatus for the sensor suite and the transmitter for the neural disruptor's clustered emitters. The cockpit is set in the 'head', crowned by a compact sensor package set low and protected, ringed with high-end optics, audio pick-ups, sensor clusters, antennae, receiver dishes. Long and wiry limbs, 'spindly' (if something several stories tall could truly be described as 'spindly'); compact musculature at the joints, overbuilt ankles and hips for sudden acceleration, violent change of direction. Elements of the build draw inspiration from the assembly of the Fencer: angular legs, built digitigrade for increased speed, stealth; left arm ends in a retractable blade assembly, sleek, narrow, optimized for reach and precision. Rather than rocket pods, the shoulders conceal retractable missle pods, apertures arranged in disciplined grids.
mechdata.sensor_suite: An enhanced predictive sensor suite to speed reactions and spot targets more quickly. (+2 Initiative)

mechdata.mech_description: A hulking, broad-shouldered biped built around mass and endurance. The torso is squat and heavily reinforced, with a recessed head unit set low between armored shoulders, marked by a pair of bright red optical sensors. Its forearms house twin 40mm rotary cannons integrated directly into the bulk of the limbs. Along the chest and abdomen, segmented armor plates conceal a torso-mounted missile rack, visible only as armored seams and recessed launch apertures that hint at stored firepower beneath. Finished in muted industrial gray broken by sharp red accent lines and stenciled marking, the Sabbath is less a mech and more a walking bunker.
mechdata.advanced_targeting_array: An advanced targeting array that increases accuracy but takes an extra moment to lock on. (+2 Accuracy, -1 Initiative)

mechdata.mech_description: Description

mechdata.color_scheme: Ashen Gray, Gunmetal Grey, Hazard Yellow
mechdata.mech_description: Intimidation is given order in every line of this omnicron's brutal architecture. The helm has an insectile feel to it, something in the sharp angles and sensor placement giving the impression of a hornet's predatory gaze. Interlocking armored plates sheath the chassis in matte shades of ashen grey and gunmetal, the yellow hazard markings all utilitarian and precise. Savage spikes at the shoulders frame the helm, adding to the overall sense of menace. It is the articulation in the plating that truly sets this Omicron apart, granting more flexibility where other heavy types may turn sluggish. The result is a mech clearly designed to withstand a beating and close in to finish the job with brutal efficiency.
Flashy color-blocking and gradients in vibrant tones of teal, purple, pink, and yellow with an iridescent sheen. Oversized fists for punching.
mechdata.enhanced_melee: Monomolecular blades, rocket-assist fists, and other enhanced melee Mech weapons.

mechdata.mech_description: Parallax is a big girl. Deep military green dominates her frame, the paint dulled by old impact marks that haven't quite been polished out. The armour plates are thick through the chest and shoulders, layered in a way that suggests endurance over elegance. Missile housings sit mounted along her back and shoulders, angled to clear the line of her shield when they fire. Said shield is fixed along her right arm as a broad slab of reinforced alloy with enough surface area to hide behind. Her left hand is intentionally left vacant and is built to grab, brace or yeet. The helm is simple and angular, broken only by a single red optic that glows steadily from within. No flare, no flash - just there . Indeed - she doesn't look fast. But she does look ready.
mechdata.sensor_suite: An enhanced predictive sensor suite to speed reactions and spot targets more quickly. (+2 Initiative)

mechdata.mech_description: Description

The scout class mech known as Umbral Wraith lives up to its name with a non-descript dark gray exterior, meant to be unassuming. Mounted on its left arm is a Gatling Blaster and it's right hand and lower arm can be reconfigured into hammer like shape for close encounters. With a stowaway position on its back and ready position on its shoulder, a railgun is ready to take out heavy opposition.
mechdata.explosive_ammo: Explosive ammo, wide-spectrum lasers, or shrapnel-laden warheads that increase damage done but have lesser penetration.

colossidata.colossus_description: Named for the Greek monsters, this humanoid Colossus has long arms and even longer fingers with almost translucent, membranous material stretched between the digits and connected to its long, thin torso. It is able to make long, gliding leaps. Its fingers are clawed and able to score through even the thickest armors.

With no wings or other means of flight, this slender and birdlike Colossus can leap into the air and fly to great altitudes and distances. Its skin is covered in a sticky, slick tarlike substance that can drip off its body and cause serious impediments to anything that touches it. Its head is narrow, ending in a hooked mouth.

The huge leathery wings of this variety of Colossus are able to easily hold it aloft. Its wings are featherless, but more reptilian in appearance than strictly chiropteran. It has a long, trailing tail that has an equal ratio to its equally long neck. It has a small head and sharp beak; a few dozen eyes cover most of its head, and each seems to be able to move independently. An orifice under its beak spits a corrosive acid that is able to eat through most materials.

Too many legs is one way this Colossus has been described. From twelve to thirty, each contact specimen has not had either the same number of legs or symmetry. Its two-bodied torso is thickly armored, and some are covered in strangely soft fibers. Acid secretes or is jettisoned from a large bump right behind the multi-eyed head. Some contact reports indicate that this acid nodule pulsates and may be better described as a sac.

This semiaquatic variety is known to use its powerful hide legs to swim in deep waters, but is otherwise found on ground. It has a long neck and a slender equine head. Its jaw is hinged like a snake and has a strong bite. The segments of its neck can expand to allow the Colossus to swallow things whole. Close study has revealed a stomach-like sac in its torso filled with corrosive acid.

This Colossus has an awkward backward shuffle that is explained by the strange arrangement of its limbs on its otherwise massive frame. Its head is disproportionate to its torso, giving it a headless appearance when at a great distance. Its primary offense is the fire-belching spines that cover its belly. When it rears up, that's a clear sign to take evasive actions.

This variety of Colossus hunches, its bowed back protecting an anterior torso covered in thick fibers or spines. It has long, knuckle-dragging front limbs with shorter back legs, each ending in misshapen hands and feet. Its tarsal digits vary in number from two to twenty. While each are distinguishable, Namnam's are always identified by the bony, club-like ends to its forelimbs.

Humanoid and multiheaded, this Colossus also features a half-dozen arms, not always symmetrically distributed bilaterally along its torso. At the end of each hand are clusters of spines, each able to be launched in a variety of numbers.

This Colossus is a canid quadruped covered in spiny armor that seems to shift and move like thick, razorsharp fur. Its jaw is long and wickedly stretched to almost beneath its sharp, pointed ears; when it unhinges, it opens wide to reveal rows and rows of teeth. It moves with a liquid quality, sometimes described as boneless despite its ability to stay upright.

Shaped more like a beetle with a squat, stumpy body, this Colossus scuttles about on six legs. Its legs are triple-jointed and end in sharp spikes that can pierce even the densest ground. It is heavily armored with wide, smiling mandibles that easily pull prey into its waiting jaws.

This quadrupedal variety is a giant mass of armor, tumors, and knotted spines. Bony protuberances curl on either side of its head, giving it a mutant ram quality. Its internal structure is almost indestructible, allowing it to batter into buildings at full speed with destructive consequences.

Standing tall and dense, this bipedal colossus is nothing more than a mass of corded muscle beneath a thickly armored carapace. While all colossus are menacing, the Cyclopean is brutally malicious in both tactics and demeanor. Its head is a bulbous mass, and a singular eye socket often flares menacing red. Its thick arms hang down long along its torso, but not quite knuckle-dragging.

A large insect-like variety, with a pair of giant claws with the ability to tear through even the thickest armor. It has an elongated head with unproportionally large mandibles clanking at its mouth. It creates its own meronymic sprogs that look like smaller versions of its holonym structure.

Like others in this directory, the Chimera variety of colossus is something of a mishmash of limbs, heads, and other body modifications. It is identifiable by the combination of being quadrapedal, possessing heads covered in hundreds of eyes, and arms bristled in giant spikes. Some possess wings, others possess barbed tails. A common attack from this variety is to either shoot or leave embedded spines in targets, both causing significant damage.

Identified by its enormous, bulbous head covered in hundreds, if not thousands of eyes, this colossus looks like a squat humanoid with a head a magnitude too large for its body. It has a lurching gait often due to the lack of lateral symmetry. The eyes that completely cover its head sometimes are found on its shoulders as well, each moving independently. It has small, razor-lined mouths on the palms of its hands and soles of its feet. These appear to be a way to provide an almost unbreakable grip on something so it can be dragged to its torso, where its stomach splays open to reveal writhing tentacles. This variety is also sometimes called Eyehorrors.

These strange worm-like colossus might be offshoots of the Tatzelwurm variety, or just share enough similarities that they could be of the same physiological mapping. They have thick, colorless bodies bristled with bony nodules along its spine and exoskeletal ribs that protect its softer underbelly. Multicolored streamers similar to planetary auroras seem to radiate from under their skin. They are capable burrowers, seeming to swim through most terrains. It is believed they perceive through echolocative song.

Sometimes bursting from the backs of other Colossus or manifesting from the dead remains of other Colossus, these flying swarms come in a variety of sizes with a number of appendages that seem unique to each swarm. They are best identified by their blob-shaped bodies and enormous heads; their thin necks end in a head that is mostly jaw. As their name suggests, they has thin membraneous wings that seem far too small for their bodies.

These long, wasp-like a long green, almost waspy bodies fly in tight, organized clutches. They are larger than meat flies, but also far more slender. They release a low whine as they fly, and they spit corrosive acid.

It is speculated that these small, almost compact merosyms are dormant in Colossus dermal strata and do not act until the Colossus dies. Upon its death, its skin splits and bursts forth with these skittering creatures. They are voracious, the whirling teeth in their abdominal mouth consuming everything in their wake. Whether it is to process dead Colossus raw material or because they are indiscriminate, they also breakdown and consume dead Colossus as well as pretty much everything else.
